fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 72 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly allows the request instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit, both > and >= must agree; they differ only at equality, revealing the off-by-one bug.
After the if-branch is skipped (else path), recent count must be strictly less than limit; if equal, the call should have been denied.
Sanity check that recent count is always comparable to limit; combined with branch logic exposes the >= vs > discrepancy.
If len(recent) equals limit the guard should deny but the buggy > condition allows it; this constraint is false exactly in the failing cases.
